21.correct: The salaries and allowances of the Governors are determined by Parliament; they are charged on the Consolidated Fund of the State.

wrong.A: The President appoints Governors but does not fix their salary.
wrong.B: The Prime Minister has no role in deciding a Governor's salary.
wrong.D: The Finance Commission deals with tax devolution, not Governors' pay.

21.fact: A Governor's emoluments cannot be reduced during the term of office.

22.correct: The salary and allowances of the Vice-President (who is ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha) are determined by Parliament by law.

wrong.A: The President does not decide the Vice-President's salary.
wrong.B: The Prime Minister has no such power.
wrong.D: The Election Commission conducts the election but does not fix the salary.

22.fact: The Vice-President draws his salary in his capacity as Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

23.correct: Yakshagana, a traditional theatre form combining dance, music and dialogue, originated in Karnataka (coastal/Malnad region).

wrong.A: Kerala's classical forms are Kathakali and Mohiniyattam.
wrong.C: Tamil Nadu is associated with Bharatanatyam and Therukoothu.
wrong.D: Andhra Pradesh is known for Kuchipudi.

23.fact: Yakshagana performances often run all night and depict stories from the epics.

24.correct: By Newton's first law (law of inertia), a body stays at rest or in uniform motion unless an applied (net external) force compels it to change its state.

wrong.A: Under balanced forces the body simply continues its state; it is an unbalanced force that changes motion.
wrong.B: Gravity is just one possible force; the law is about any net force.
wrong.D: Removing friction does not by itself cause change; a net applied force does.

24.fact: Newton's first law defines inertia - the tendency of a body to resist changes in its motion.

25.correct: Ruskin Bond won the Sahitya Akademi Award in 1992 for his collection 'Our Trees Still Grow in Dehra'.

wrong.A: 'The Blue Umbrella' is a famous novella but not the Akademi-winning work.
wrong.C: 'Angry River' is another of his children's books.
wrong.D: 'Delhi Is Not Far' is a separate work.

25.fact: Ruskin Bond was also awarded the Padma Shri (1999) and Padma Bhushan (2014).

26.correct: The Narmada flows from east to west (through a rift valley between the Vindhya and Satpura ranges) into the Arabian Sea.

wrong.B: It does not flow west to east.
wrong.C: It does not flow north to south.
wrong.D: It does not flow south to north.

26.fact: The Narmada and the Tapti are the major peninsular rivers that flow westward into the Arabian Sea.

27.correct: The Northern Plains have the most fertile soil in India, built from rich alluvium deposited by the Himalayan rivers.

wrong.A: Coastal plains are fertile but smaller and less uniformly rich.
wrong.C: The peninsular plateau has older, less fertile soils.
wrong.D: The Himalayan mountains have thin, rocky mountain soils.

27.fact: The fertile alluvial Northern Plains support intensive agriculture and dense population.

28.correct: The neutron was discovered in 1932 by James Chadwick, for which he received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1935.

wrong.A: Ernest Rutherford discovered the nucleus and the proton, and predicted the neutron, but did not discover it.
wrong.B: Niels Bohr gave the model of the atom.
wrong.D: Wolfgang Pauli proposed the neutrino and the exclusion principle.

28.fact: The neutron is a neutral subatomic particle found in the nucleus along with protons.

29.correct: In the Ease of Doing Business framework, the 'Getting Electricity' indicator measures the cost, time and number of procedures required to obtain an electricity connection.

wrong.A: The number of electrified households is not what this indicator measures.
wrong.B: National electricity generation is a separate energy statistic.
wrong.D: Energy efficiency is unrelated to this indicator.

29.fact: It also factors in the reliability of supply and transparency of tariffs.

30.correct: Aditya-L1, India's first dedicated solar mission, began regular science observations and released its early datasets, studying the Sun from the L1 point.

wrong.A: AstroSat-2 is not yet an operational solar mission.
wrong.C: Chandrayaan-3 is a lunar mission, not a solar observatory.
wrong.D: XPoSat studies cosmic X-ray sources, not the Sun's regular observations described.

30.fact: Aditya-L1 orbits the Sun-Earth Lagrange point L1, about 1.5 million km from Earth, for an uninterrupted view of the Sun.

31.correct: Per the answer key, the Judo Federation of India was officially established and affiliated to the International Judo Federation in 1965.

wrong.A: 1905 is far too early for organised judo administration in India.
wrong.B: 1929 is not the founding year given.
wrong.D: 1986 is later than the establishment year.

31.fact: Judo, a Japanese martial art, became an Olympic sport for men in 1964 (Tokyo).

32.correct: Despite the lander's failure, the Chandrayaan-2 orbiter remained successful - it continues to orbit the Moon and send back valuable scientific data.

wrong.A: Chandrayaan-2 studied the Moon, not Venus.
wrong.D: It was a lunar mission, not India's first Mars mission (that was Mangalyaan).

32.fact: The Chandrayaan-2 orbiter carries a high-resolution camera and instruments still mapping the lunar surface.

33.correct: Sir M. Visvesvaraya is regarded as the father of Indian (economic) planning; his book 'Planned Economy for India' (1934) outlined a structured plan for development.

wrong.A: Dadabhai Naoroji is known for the 'drain of wealth' theory.
wrong.B: Jawaharlal Nehru championed planning and set up the Planning Commission but is not called its father.
wrong.C: P.C. Mahalanobis designed the model for the Second Five-Year Plan.

33.fact: Visvesvaraya, a celebrated engineer, is honoured on Engineer's Day (15 September) in India.

34.correct: The industrial licensing system was introduced in 1951 through the Industries (Development and Regulation) Act.

wrong.A: 1948 was the first Industrial Policy Resolution, not the licensing Act.
wrong.C: 1956 was the second Industrial Policy Resolution.
wrong.D: 1969 was the year of bank nationalization and the MRTP Act.

34.fact: This licensing regime (the 'Licence Raj') was largely dismantled by the 1991 reforms.

35.correct: The IndiaAI Mission is the artificial-intelligence programme launched by the Government of India (approved 2024) to build AI compute, datasets and skilling capacity.

wrong.B: 'Digital AI India Plan' is not the official name.
wrong.C: 'Bharat AI Strategy' is not the launched mission's name.
wrong.D: 'AI India Vision 2040' is not the official programme.

35.fact: The IndiaAI Mission aims to develop a sovereign AI ecosystem, including affordable computing power for startups and researchers.

36.correct: The main objective of the Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management (FRBM) Act, 2003 is to reduce the fiscal deficit and ensure prudent, disciplined public finances.

wrong.A: Controlling money supply is the job of the RBI's monetary policy.
wrong.B: Managing exports is the role of trade policy.
wrong.C: Promoting cooperative banking is unrelated to the FRBM Act.

36.fact: The FRBM Act sets targets for the government to limit borrowing and bring down the deficit over time.

37.correct: The Merchandise Exports from India Scheme (MEIS) provided financial incentives (duty-credit scrips) to promote exports, including from Special Economic Zones.

wrong.B: There is no scheme by the name 'National Export Insurance Scheme' for this purpose.
wrong.C: 'Prime Minister Export Fund' is not the incentive scheme.
wrong.D: 'Foreign Trade Investment Programme' is not the relevant scheme.

37.fact: MEIS was later replaced by the WTO-compliant RoDTEP (Remission of Duties and Taxes on Exported Products) scheme.

38.correct: As per the Economic Survey 2024-25, social-service expenditure recorded an annual growth rate of about 15% between FY21 and FY25.

wrong.C: 21% is higher than the figure reported.

38.fact: Social-service expenditure covers areas like education, health and social welfare.

39.correct: Rani Lakshmibai was strongly supported by Tatya Tope, who fought alongside her against the British at Jhansi and Gwalior in 1857-58.

wrong.A: Rani Avantibai Lodhi led a separate revolt in Ramgarh.
wrong.B: Bahadur Shah Zafar was the symbolic leader at Delhi.
wrong.C: Begum Hazrat Mahal led the revolt at Lucknow.

39.fact: Tatya Tope was captured and executed in 1859; Rani Lakshmibai died fighting near Gwalior in 1858.

40.correct: Subhas Chandra Bose organised and led the Indian National Army (INA, Azad Hind Fauj) to fight for India's independence with the slogan 'Dilli Chalo'.

wrong.A: 'Swaraj Sena' was not the name of Bose's army.
wrong.D: 'Bharat Mukti Sena' is not the INA.

40.fact: The INA included the Rani of Jhansi Regiment, one of the first all-women combat units in Asia.
